column2: card-specific counter-timing on Anarch Convert's own ability sequence; no general principle beyond this card
column4: A counter is added when {Anarch Convert} enters play, before using his ability. If he uses his ability to make a non-anarch vampire anarch, a second counter is added. [ANK 20210410]

column2: reminder that moving blood beyond a vampire's capacity caps at the target and the excess goes to the blood bank; restates a basic capacity rule any fluent judge knows; nearest 5.1
column4: Can be used to move more blood than the target can hold: the excess goes to the blood bank. [ANK 20170804]

column2: confirms the plain reading of "current action card" (whichever action card is active); restates evident wording, no transfer
column4: Applies to the current action card, if any. [LSJ 20091002]

column2: card's own open-bid auction procedure; not a rulebook mechanic (only "similar to" a referendum); fails transfer — nearest 3.7.5
column4: The bidding is conducted in an open format, similar to a referendum. The player who played the card conducts the auction. [LSJ 19990104]

column2: specific combo of gaining pool without needing to cancel an [aus] card; fails transfer — nearest 1.8
column4: [DAI][OBF] Can be used to gain a pool after a successful action, without cancelling an [aus] card. [LSJ 20100902-2]

column2: card's own participate/pass procedure for its titled-vampire blood transfer; fails transfer — nearest 5.8
column4: The acting Methuselah participates, and a Methuselah can pass as many times as they want. If only the acting Methuselah has a ready titled vampire, they can use the effect to transfer as much blood as they want to their pool. [LSJ 20051026]

column2: card's own roaming-retainer restriction when acquired via a "this vampire employs" ability rather than the normal employ action; fails transfer — nearest 3.7.4
column4: If grabbed by a "this vampire employs" effect (not the normal employ action), the vampire receiving Ilomba does not get to move Ilomba on to a new target. [LSJ 20090824] [LSJ 20051121]

column2: trivial discipline-tag matching (a Potence strike is enhanced by Potence-boosters); fails non-obviousness — nearest 4.3
column4: [pot] Is a strike that requires Potence, so would be enhanced by {Increased Strength}. [LSJ 20030611]

column2: card-specific targeting of a resourceless minion; no stated general rule beyond ordinary "no effect" consequences — nearest 3.4
column4: Can use his action on a vampire with no blood. [RTR 20010711]

column2: card's own counter-tracking mechanism, scoped to its own ability; fails transfer — nearest 1.6
column4: No counter is added if the vampire uses another game effect (eg. {Ian Forestal}, another {Infernal Familiar}) to play a card that requires a Discipline they do not have. [RTR 20020501] [LSJ 20100517]

column2: reminder that lock restricts actions, not card plays; fails non-obviousness — nearest 1.5
column4: Can be played by a locked vampire. [LSJ 19980210]

column2: obvious consequence of unlocking resetting ability to act, a separate action from the Jack's own; fails non-obviousness — nearest 3.7.3
column4: Does not prevent the acting vampire from playing the equipment or retainer directly this turn if he unlocks (not the same action). [ANK 20180817]

column2: this card's own fixed life stat; no general principle — nearest 5.5
column4: His "starting life amount" is 1 ({Vagabond Mystic} cannot give hime more life). [LSJ 20030520-2]

column2: trivial reading of its own "or" clause between two clan names; fails non-obviousness — nearest 1.6
column4: Only requires one clan or the other (Tremere or Tremere antitribu) to be played. [ANK 20190203]

column2: describes only its own targeting/resolution outcome on an empty vampire; fails transfer — nearest 4.4
column4: Does not damage vampires: an empty vampire will simply attempt to burn blood and fail. [TOM 19951212-2]

column2: card's own equip/counter interaction; fails transfer — nearest 1.12
column4: Equipping it does not put a counter on it [LSJ 20100207-2]

column2: clarifies the card grants only an option, not the sect/infernal status itself; narrow reading specific to this card — nearest 1.1
column4: Does not make the vampire Sabbat nor infernal: it merely gives the option to such vampires. [PIB 20150809]

column2: trivial reading of "any" as "all"; fails non-obviousness, cf. Great Symposium.1 — nearest 1.2
column4: Any crypt cards means all of them. [LSJ 20080229]

column2: card's own point-allocation limit; fails transfer — nearest 6.5
column4: Cannot allocate 4 points to a single Methuselah, but you can allocate points to yourself. [LSJ 20000622]

column2: card's own allocation-exceeding-pool rule; fails transfer — nearest 6.5
column4: Can allocate more points than a Methuselah has pool. [LSJ 20020819]

column2: card-specific steal-strike targeting a low-resource minion; fails non-obviousness — nearest 4.4
column4: [VIC] The strike to steal 2 blood can be used on a minion with less than 2 blood or life. [RTR 20010711]

column2: card-specific requirement that a vampire exist to hunt on; fails transfer beyond Legacy of Caine's vampire-targeted hunt mode
column4: The vampire cannot hunt if there are no vampires at all. [LSJ 19970224]

column2: card-specific rule for how Lock's own condition is satisfied across several targets; fails transfer, no other card shares this "any one target suffices" wording
column4: Once in play, applies to an action if any one of its targets would be enough to enable his effect. [LSJ 20080809-2] [KOT 20081119]

column2: specific two-card interaction (Madness Network's out-of-turn grant meeting Mask's masking restriction); fails transfer beyond this pairing
column4: A non-Malkavian cannot use {Mask of a Thousand Faces} to assume the action of a Malkavian on someone else's turn. [LSJ 19981007]

column2: card-specific restriction on Madness Network's own out-of-turn action grant (forced hunt when no blood); fails transfer to other cards
column4: If a Malkavian has no blood, he may declare an action during another Methuselah's turn but, if he does, that action must be a hunt action. [LSJ 20060508]

column2: idiosyncratic bidding/auction mechanic specific to this card; fails transfer to non-auction cards
column4: Bidding continues until noone wants to bid. You do not participate in the bid. [LSJ 20030604]

column2: narrow interaction of burning during an action whose targets only partially overlap a prior action's; fails transfer
column4: Can be burned during an action that targets only part of the Methuselah that were targeted by the first action. [LSJ 20100527]

column2: card-specific equipment self-targeting restriction; fails transfer beyond this ring
column4: The bearer cannot use [ser] or [dem] cards on themselves (eg. {Mummify}). [LSJ 20080212]

column2: restates the ordinary control principle (only the controller of the acting minion may play this) — obvious even to a fluent judge; fails non-obviousness
column4: Can only be played by the controller of the acting minion. [LSJ 19990425]

column2: narrow masking-eligibility exception specific to equipment-granted actions; fails transfer
column4: Cannot be used to take over an action granted by an equipment the masked minion has, even if the masking vampire also has the same equipment. [LSJ 20020926]

column2: narrow timing window specific to Mask's own masking mechanic; fails transfer
column4: Can be used after the action is blocked and before the block resolution (eg. {Venenation} can be played both before and after). [LSJ 20010803]

column2: narrow interaction of masking with unblockable effects; fails transfer
column4: Can be used after blocks are declined if the masked minion was benefiting from an effect that made him unblockable, even if the masking minion does not benefit from the same effect (eg. {Toreador Grand Ball}, {Nakhthorheb}). [PIB 20150924-1] [PIB 20150930]

column2: restates Mask's own requirement (unlocked vampire) colliding with locked-minion actions; fails transfer
column4: Cannot be used to take over an action requiring a locked minion like {Force of Will}, since Mask requires an unlocked vampire. [LSJ 20020927]

column2: obvious requirement that a valid target exist for the effect; fails non-obviousness
column4: Cannot be used if there is no equipment to steal. [RTR 19980928]

column2: burn-option trigger tied to vampires with [mal] being in torpor is this card's own condition-check; fails transfer, nearest 5.3 (torpor) but that doesn't own conditional burn options
column4: The burn option can be used if all your vampires with [mal] are in torpor. [LSJ 20091203]

column2: question/answer-card mechanic specific to Monocle of Clarity's own text (answer must reflect best knowledge); taxonomy explicitly names this cluster as a rejected single-card topic mislabelled G.
column4: The answer must be "yes" or "no", to the best of the player's knowledge. [LSJ 20021122-4]

column2: same Monocle of Clarity question-mechanic cluster (agreeing on term meanings before answering); rejected single-card topic per taxonomy.
column4: The owner and target Methuselah have to agree on the meanings of the terms of the question (eg. "bounce" card). In case of doubt, they should discuss to agree on a shared meaning for those terms before answering the question. [LSJ 20030908]

column2: same cluster (inexact answers for unverifiable elements, not judge-checkable); rejected single-card topic per taxonomy.
column4: The answer may be inexact when pertaining to an unverifiable element, such as the deck's content. A judge cannot be called to check the deck content. [LSJ 20021121-3] [PIB 20111213]

column2: same cluster (must indicate invoking the question ability); rejected single-card topic per taxonomy. Nearest 1.5.
column4: The owner must indicate that they are using the Monocle to ask the question, by whatever method passes for such an indication. [LSJ 20021121-4]

column2: same cluster (conjunctive questions answerable as a whole); rejected single-card topic per taxonomy.
column4: You can ask a question about multiple things using conjunctions, as long as the question as a whole can be answered by "yes" or "no". [LSJ 20021212]

column2: restates this card's own printed play-restriction (controller of the acting minion only); no transfer beyond the card's text
column4: [DOM] Can only be played by the controller of the acting minion. [LSJ 19990425]

column2: card-specific restriction on what the played [dom] card grants; fails transfer, no other card's ruling changes
column4: [DOM] Cannot be used to let the target play a [dom] action card. [LSJ 20100527]

column2: !WORDING unclear whose search this binds ("each Methuselah" vs. the searching player); as written it restates that all matching copies must be found, which fails non-obviousness
column4: Each Methuselah must find as many copies of the vampire as possible when searching. [LSJ 20050611]

column2: restates this event's own printed lock-or-burn requirement each unlock phase; fails non-obviousness, no transfer beyond this card
column4: You cannot end your unlock phase or pass the impulse if you control a minion you haven't locked or burned a card for. [ANK 20210322]

column2: lists this card's own exemptions (mortal allies, high-capacity vampires); card-specific scope, no transfer
column4: Mortal allies are not affected, and vampires with capacity above the number of Gehenna cards in play are not affected. [LSJ 20040415]

column2: restates this weapon's own printed close-range-only restriction; fails non-obviousness
column4: Both strikes are only effective at close range. [ANK 20220814-2]

column2: states this card's own Anarch-requirement status; card-specific fact, no transfer to other cards
column4: Is not a card requiring an Anarch. [LSJ 20080812]

column2: procedural mechanics specific to this card's mass equipment-redistribution effect; no evidence of a recurring template, fails transfer; nearest 3.7.3
column4: Choose for each equipment which minion will receive it, then transfer all the equipment accordingly. [ANK 20190717-1]

column2: restates the card's own Laibon clan requirement; fails non-obviousness
column4: The effects do not apply if the vampire is not Laibon. [ANK 20221208]

column2: timing of this card's own Orun-burn cost relative to its special enter-combat action; card-specific, fails transfer; nearest 4.9
column4: If the Laibon performs a Ⓓ action to enter combat, they burn one Orun only after combat. [ANK 20240809]

column2: narrow interaction of this card's granted Auspex block with Sniper Rifle's own strike requirement; fails transfer beyond this exact combination; nearest 4.3
column4: If Auspex [aus] was used to block him, the blocker can use an effect to set the range, but not a {Sniper Rifle} as it would require him to strike with it, which he cannot. [LSJ 20100827]

column2: specific to this card's own repeated-Auspex-use case; nearest 4.3 (strikes) but establishes no rule beyond this card
column4: If no Auspex [aus] was used previously, an Auspex [aus] strike can be used (eg. {Charismatic Aura}). [ANK 20210614]

column2: restates the default "must have a legal target" requirement; fails non-obviousness
column4: Cannot be played if there is no legal target (no other Methuselah with more pool than you). [ANK 20191228]

column2: restates this card's own printed search restriction (no discipline already at superior on all vampires); fails transfer
column4: You cannot choose a Discipline card that all your vampires already have at superior. [LSJ 20031209]

column2: Personal Scourge's own protection scope (excludes self-inflicted weapon damage); fails transfer — no recurring "self-inflicted damage" formula to generalize from; nearest 4.5, which owns prevention scope generally, not this card's specific exclusion
column4: Does not protect the target from self-inflicted damage from a weapon. [LSJ 19980216]

column2: Portrait's own capacity-reveal/bled-this-turn condition fizzling the remainder of its effect; fails transfer — no recurring template for this effect chain; nearest 2.5, which does not cover conditional effect fizzling on this card
column4: If the revealed vampire is between 7 and 10 capacity and the acting vampire has bled this turn already, he simply unlocks and the remainder of the effect is lost. [LSJ 20021118]

column2: interaction specific to this named card pair ({Church of the Order of St. Blaise} + Powerbase: Barranquilla); fails transfer — no other pairing generalizes from it; nearest 1.12 (cards on cards / counters)
column4: {Church of the Order of St. Blaise} may be used to add a counter to it before it burns if it has zero counters. [LSJ 20050105-1]

column2: Powerbase: Berlin's own blood-source bookkeeping for its action; fails transfer — specific to this card's action; nearest 6.1, which does not own blood-source attribution
column4: Blood moved to it by a Ventrue action comes from the Ventrue. [LSJ 19970414]

column2: Powerbase: New York's own action-eligibility restriction (cannot steal from your own powerbase); fails transfer — specific to this card's special action; nearest 3.1
column4: The action to steal the blood from your powerbase cannot be attempted by your own vampires. [RTR 19980928]

column2: Principia Discordia's own targeting clarification (zero-blood vampire remains a valid target); fails transfer — answers only whether this card can target such a vampire; nearest 3.1
column4: [qui] can target a vampire with no blood. [RTR 20010711]

column2: same as .1, [ser] version of the same card's own targeting clarification; fails transfer; nearest 3.1
column4: [ser] can target a vampire with no blood.[RTR 20010711]

column2: Psyche!'s own retrieval eligibility via {Marthe Dizier}, reversing an earlier ruling; fails transfer — specific to this card interaction; nearest 1.11
column4: Can be retrieved by {Marthe Dizier} (reversal of previous rulings). [PIB 20111208]

column2: Pulled Fangs' own counter-removal condition (two actions by any vampires); fails transfer — specific to this card's removal mechanic; nearest 3.5
column4: A total of two actions must be performed (by whomever) to remove it. Vampires A and B can both take the action, then it is removed. [ANK 20170202-2]

column2: Reanimated Corpse's own pathos-counter mechanic on burn/return; fails transfer — specific to this card's counter type; nearest 1.12
column4: Looses all its pathos counters if it is burned. If it is brought back with X being zero (eg. {Compel the Spirit}), it has no pathos counter and burns immediately. [LSJ 20071015]

column2: Red Question's own burn-option restriction (blocked by a specific torpor/title vampire state); fails transfer — specific to this card's burn clause; nearest 5.8
column4: The burn option cannot be used if you have a non-titled, non-Anarch vampire in torpor. [LSJ 20091204]

column2: Redistribution's own targeting restriction tied to Blood Brother + uncontrolled status; fails transfer — specific to this card/clan combination; nearest 6.7
column4: Cannot target an uncontrolled Blood Brother. [LSJ 20061109]

column2: Reformation's own no-valid-target restriction; fails non-obviousness — a card requiring a target trivially cannot be played absent one; nearest 3.1
column4: [chi] Cannot be used if there is no equipment to destroy. [RTR 19980928]

column2: Rego Motum's own restatement that prevention needs damage present to prevent; fails non-obviousness — prevention is inherently reactive to inflicted damage; nearest 4.5
column4: Cannot be used if there is no damage to prevent. [LSJ 20001114]

column2: two-card ordering interaction (Relentless Reaper vs {Psyche!}); fails transfer — no general rule beyond this specific pairing. Nearest 4.9, which doesn't own ordering between arbitrary effect pairs
column4: [THN] Cannot be played after [CEL] {Psyche!}. When played before, combat continues and {Psyche!} can only be played at the end of it. [ANK 20191219]

column2: reaction usable at the moment {Form of Corruption} is put into play, protecting only against that card; fails transfer beyond this pairing. Nearest 2.1, which covers generic sequencing, not this abnormal-entry reaction window
column4: Can be used when a {Form of Corruption} is put into play to protect that vampire from that {Form of Corruption}. [LSJ 20031223]

column2: confirms lock status alone doesn't bar targeting; fails non-obviousness — default targeting rules already establish this absent an explicit readiness restriction. Nearest 5.2
column4: Can target a locked minion. [ANK 20170303]

column2: clarifies this card's political effect does not grant control of the opponent's minion; fails transfer — a negative scope statement about this card's own text. Nearest 6.2
column4: Does not allow to take control of the opponent. [ANK 20200925] [ANK 20211105]

column2: card-specific usage condition (cannot use the burn option while controlling a ready Kiasyd); fails transfer — applies only to this card's own gating clause. Nearest 1.6
column4: The burn option can only be used if you do not control a ready Kiasyd (no matter the presence or absence of a valid target). [ANK 20240610]

column2: confirms superior discipline doesn't change the card's stated prevention fraction; fails non-obviousness — card text controls and superior doesn't implicitly scale printed values. Nearest 4.5
column4: [VIS] Superior still prevents only half of the current strike. [PIB 20120116]

column2: describes this card's own targeting placement (played on the vampire about to take the damage); fails transfer — restates the card's own text. Nearest 4.5
column4: Is played on the vampire that would receive the aggravated damage. [RTR 19980623]

column2: confirms no default restriction against choosing the same target vampire multiple times across copies; fails non-obviousness — absent an explicit restriction this is already assumed. Nearest 3.1
column4: The same vampire can be chosen multiple times if multiple copies are played. [LSJ 19970707]
